title: Match Your AI Strategy to Your Organization's Reality
content:
Artificial intelligence is often hailed as a game changer, but too many firms discover that their bold AI pilots eventually collapse. These failures usually happen because their operating models simply cannot support the new technology. AI success depends less on the raw algorithms than on the fit between a company's ambitions and its organizational reality. Leaders should evaluate themselves along two key dimensions: value-chain control and technological breadth. Value-chain control measures a company's influence over the journey from idea to market, while technological breadth spans the range of integrated technologies.

Companies pursuing vertical integration often excel at unlocking novel connections and linking data across silos. However, even firms with strong operations can stumble when their ambition outpaces their execution capabilities. Collaborative ecosystems offer another approach, especially for those lacking direct market control. In these complex environments, AI success comes from partnering strategically rather than attempting to go it alone. Organizations must share innovation risks, infrastructure, and expertise to truly harness AI's potential.

Platform leadership requires shaping the norms rather than just adapting to them. Companies at the apex of both dimensions create the underlying infrastructure while simultaneously building products. This is the domain of orchestration, which involves setting standards and designing systems that others actively want to build upon. Across all these strategies, the defining challenge remains fundamentally human rather than technical. When employees feel like participants in shaping the future, they stop fearing AI and instead help build it.
